extra.reasoning: The property requires NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). rec_drug_a is set true only by r1 (priority 50), whose antecedent is a conjunction of 10 conditions including polypharmacy. rec_drug_b is set true by r5 (priority 50) whose antecedent is just polypharmacy. r1's antecedent therefore strictly implies r5's antecedent: any input that fires r1 must also fire r5. With all 10 of r1's conjuncts set true (polypharmacy, anticoagulated, ckd_dialysis, heart_failure, diabetes, immunosuppressed, asthma, g6pd_deficiency, hyperkalemia, low_ejection_fraction), r1 fires -> rec_drug_a := true and r5 fires (polypharmacy true) -> rec_drug_b := true. No conflict-resolution mechanism intervenes: r1 is the sole writer of rec_drug_a and r5 is the sole writer of rec_drug_b, so each output takes its rule's value (no defaults applied since both are set). Rules r2-r4 only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant; the three remaining inputs (prior_gi_bleed, elderly, bradycardia) are set false. Both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are jointly true, so the mutual-exclusion property is violated.
